questions.question: Explain the Fixed Price contract type and identify two advantages and two disadvantages.
keyPoints: Fixed Price: Vendor agrees to deliver at predetermined cost
Advantages: Budget certainty for client, incentive for vendor to be efficient
Disadvantages: Vendor may cut corners, inflexible for scope changes, vendor bears cost overrun risk

questions.question: What is a Service Level Agreement (SLA)? List any four components that should be included in an SLA.
keyPoints: SLA is a formal agreement defining expected service standards
Components: 1) Service scope 2) Performance metrics 3) Response times 4) Uptime requirements 5) Penalties 6) Reporting requirements

questions.question: Describe the key elements that should be covered in a software contract.
keyPoints: Key elements: Project scope/SOW, Deliverables, Timeline/Milestones, Payment terms, Acceptance criteria, Warranty terms, IP rights, Confidentiality/NDA, Termination clauses, Dispute resolution

questions.question: Explain the concept of Risk Allocation in contract management. What are the?
keyPoints: Risk allocation distributes project risks between client and vendor
Principles: 1) Risk to party who can best manage it 2) Risk to party who causes it 3) Party bearing risk should receive compensation 4) Balance risk and reward

questions.question: What is a Change Request? Explain the typical process for handling changes in a software contract.
keyPoints: Change Request: Formal proposal to modify project scope/requirements
Process: 1) Client submits change request 2) Vendor assesses impact (cost/time) 3) Negotiation 4) Client approval 5) Contract amendment 6) Implementation

questions.question: Differentiate between Time and Materials (T&M) and Cost Plus contracts.
keyPoints: T&M: Client pays for actual time and materials, budget uncertain, flexible for changes
Cost Plus: Vendor reimbursed for actual costs plus fixed fee or percentage, vendor has no cost risk, less incentive for efficiency

questions.question: Explain the importance of Intellectual Property (IP) rights in software contracts. What are the common IP arrangements?
keyPoints: IP rights determine ownership of code, documentation, and innovations
Common arrangements: 1) Client owns IP (custom development) 2) Vendor retains IP (product licenses) 3) Joint ownership 4) Pre-existing IP (vendor keeps), New IP (client gets)

questions.question: Discuss the contract selection process in software engineering. How would you choose the appropriate contract type for a project with poorly defined requirements versus a well-defined small project?
keyPoints: Contract selection depends on: Project complexity, Requirement stability, Budget certainty needs, Risk tolerance, Vendor capability
Poorly defined requirements: Choose Time and Materials or Cost Plus for flexibility
Well-defined small project: Choose Fixed Price for budget certainty and vendor efficiency incentive
Justify with examples

questions.question: Explain the contract lifecycle management process. Discuss the activities involved in each phase from initiation to termination.
keyPoints: Phases: 1) Initiation: Define requirements, issue RFP 2) Negotiation: Evaluate proposals, negotiate terms 3) Execution: Sign contract, begin work 4) Monitoring: Track performance against SLA 5) Amendment: Handle changes 6) Termination/Renewal: Complete or extend contract
Each phase has specific activities and deliverables
Emphasize importance of documentation and mutual agreement

questions.question: What is vendor management? Discuss the key activities involved in managing software vendors and ensuring contract compliance.
keyPoints: Vendor Management: Systematic approach to selecting, monitoring, and managing vendors

Key activities: 1) Vendor selection (RFP process, evaluation criteria) 2) Performance monitoring (SLA tracking, KPIs) 3) Communication management 4) Risk management 5) Contract enforcement 6) Relationship management
Tools: Vendor scorecards, regular audits, performance reviews

questions.question: Draft a comprehensive checklist of important clauses that must be included in a software development contract.
keyPoints: Essential clauses: 1) Scope of work (SOW) 2) Deliverables and milestones 3) Payment terms and schedule 4) Acceptance criteria and testing 5) Warranty and support 6) Intellectual Property rights 7) Confidentiality/NDA 8) Termination clauses 9) Limitation of liability 10) Dispute resolution 11) Change management process 12) Service Level Agreements 13) Penalty and bonus provisions 14) Insurance requirements 15) Compliance and regulatory requirements
